title: festuca ovina subvar. villosa
intro: Festuca ovina subvar. villosa, also known as woolly sheep's fescue, is a subvariety of the Festuca ovina species, a type of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subvar. villosa include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems and digestive issues.
